text: Low intensity endurance training at lactate threshold (60 min, 5x/week for 6 weeks) increases skeletal muscle capillary density, with capillary-to-fiber ratio improving from 1.97 to 2.49.
Six weeks of low intensity training at lactate threshold improves VO2max by 5% and lactate threshold VO2 by 27%.
Low intensity training at lactate threshold produces substantial aerobic adaptations without any change in body weight.

Gains in lactate threshold VO2 are disproportionately larger than gains in VO2max from low intensity training, suggesting submaximal aerobic capacity is more responsive to easy training than maximal capacity.
Increased muscle capillary density from low intensity training is associated with improved blood lipoprotein profiles, including higher HDL2-C and lower LDL-C/HDL-C ratio.
specific_value: cap/fiber vs HDL2-C r = 0.82 (p < 0.01), vs LDL-C/HDL-C r = -0.61 (p < 0.05)
